About RFTT

Where Lived Experience Becomes Structured Knowledge

Book Synopsis

The Book That Introduces the RFTT Story

Rising from the Township — Inspiring Stories of South African Entrepreneurs

By Dr Steven Zwane & Yamkela Khoza Tywakadi

10 inspiring South African entrepreneur journeysTownship enterprise stories turned into structured learningThe foundation of the wider RFTT knowledge system

This book codifies 10 real township enterprise journeys into compelling narratives that reveal the resilience, ingenuity, and strategic thinking behind South Africa's most inspiring grassroots entrepreneurs.

More than a collection of stories, it is the foundation on which the wider RFTT knowledge system is built, showing how lived entrepreneurial experience can become structured learning, institutional insight, and scalable frameworks for education, research, and enterprise development.

Get Your CopyDownload Synopsis

Founder

Dr Steven Zwane

Founder of RFTTCo-Author of Rising from the TownshipSenior Lecturer at GIBSFounder of YLED

Dr Steven Zwane works at the intersection of multiple disciplines, bridging academic rigour with entrepreneurial practice to create systems that transform how township enterprise is understood, taught, and scaled.

Works at the Intersection of:

Entrepreneurship Education
Township Enterprise Development
Shared Value Strategy
Knowledge System Design
Youth Entrepreneurial Intention-to-Action Research

Published Case Studies

His cases have been featured in Ivey Publishing and Emerald Emerging Markets Case Studies, making African township entrepreneurship accessible to global university classrooms.

“To transform township entrepreneurship stories into codified knowledge systems that can be taught, scaled, researched, and embedded within universities, corporates, and policy frameworks.”

— Dr Steven Zwane, Vision for RFTT

Mission

To ensure township enterprises are studied, systematised, and institutionalised — moving from informal resilience to structured, scalable knowledge that shapes education, industry, and policy.

Platform Purpose

Codifying Township Enterprise Into Scalable Systems

RFTT is a knowledge creation and entrepreneurship education platform that codifies township enterprise journeys into structured, research-informed systems. We convert lived experience into frameworks that can be taught, scaled, and institutionalised.

Academic Case Studies

Published through Ivey Publishing and Emerald Emerging Markets Case Studies for global university classrooms.

High School Learning Models

Age-appropriate entrepreneurship workbooks aligned with South Africa's national curriculum policy goals.

University Curriculum

Full course modules, teaching notes, and assessment frameworks for higher education institutions.

Corporate Shared Value Frameworks

Structured enterprise development and CSI programmes grounded in real township enterprise data.

Policy-Relevant Research

Evidence-based insights informing entrepreneurship education policy at national and provincial levels.

The Transition

From Inspiration to Impact

01

Inspiration

Capturing lived entrepreneurial journeys from the township.

02

Instruction

Converting stories into structured teaching and learning content.

03

Institutionalisation

Embedding knowledge into universities, schools, corporates, and policy.

04

Impact

Creating scalable systems that drive entrepreneurial capability nationally.

01

Inspiration

Capturing lived entrepreneurial journeys from the township.

02

Instruction

Converting stories into structured teaching and learning content.

03

Institutionalisation

Embedding knowledge into universities, schools, corporates, and policy.

04

Impact

Creating scalable systems that drive entrepreneurial capability nationally.
